sections.title: While finding a book for them

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: Have you found my book yet? '[book name]'

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: It's just typical of the Arceuus snobs that their Librarian's too good to help customers like me. They ought to be grateful to you for doing her job for her.

sections.title: While finding a book for someone else

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: I'll grab you later when you're not busy helping someone else.

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: Thanks, I'll get on with reading it. Meanwhile, you can have this other book of mine - I won't be wanting to read it again.

sections.title: Talking to them after giving them the book

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: Thanks for finding the book. I'll have to think up what I need next.

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: Can you help me find a book I want? That snotty Arceuus Librarian won't even say hello, never mind actually helping me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure, what book do you want?

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: I think I'd like to read: '[book name]'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: I'm about to join the crew of a cargo ship, trading goods with the eastern lands. I hear our voyages can last for weeks, especially if the winds are slack.

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.text: I don't want to run out of stuff to talk about with the other sailors, so I'm doing plenty of background reading before we sail. Will you find my book, please?
